REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. COMPLAINT STATES SUFFICIENT FACT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE LANDLORD IS NOT AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY TO THIS ACTION AS CURRENTLY ALLEGED. DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION IS NOT CONSIDERED ON THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. PLAINTIFF'S LATE-FILED OPPOSITION IS STRICKEN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF JOHN SINCLAIR'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS. THE "COUNTERCLAIM" (STARTING AT PAGE 11 LINE 2) AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGE (AT PAGE 13, LINE 16) ARE STRICKEN FROM THE AMENDED ANSWER. AMENDED ANSWER SHALL OTHERWISE REMAIN THE OPERATIVE ANSWER. DEFENDANT SHALL HAVE 10-DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO RE-FILE HIS "COUNTERCLAIM" AS A SEPARATE CROSS-COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Jun-16-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. Complaint states sufficient fact to constitute a cause of action and the landlord is not an indispensable party to this action as currently alleged. Defendant's Declaration is not considered on the motion for judgment on the Pleadings. Plaintiff's late-filed opposition is stricken.

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

John M. Sinclair; 415-780-3165; plaintiff in pro per, appearing on their own behalf.
Ken Keisler; defendant in pro per, appearing on their own behalf.

Plaintiff contests the portion of the court's tentative ruling which allows for defendant to file a cross-complaint. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN SINCLAIR'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is GRANTED as follows. The "Counterclaim" (starting at page 11 line 2) against Plaintiff and the prayer for punitive damage (at page 13, line 16) are stricken from the Amended Answer. Amended Answer shall otherwise remain the operative answer. Defendant shall have 10-days from notice of entry of order to re-file his "counterclaim" as a separate Cross-Complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
